title: On Measurement as Infrastructure
text:
Measurement is not a sidebar to science�it is the infrastructure that makes discovery legible. When we get measurement wrong, we silently corrupt every downstream inference.
This essay outlines a pragmatic view of measurement: treat it as a public good, document its assumptions, and design it for reuse.

title: Marcus Harris
text:
I build tools and theories for reliable measurement in psychology and education. My work focuses on psychometric modeling, meta-research, and open, reproducible methods.
Research Focus - Measurement invariance and fairness - Meta-analytic methods and bias detection - Bayesian and multilevel psychometrics
Current Work - Developing simulation tools for power and reliability in scale validation - Auditing common practices in scale development across subfields - Building open teaching materials for measurement theory
